channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to watch New Zealand v England ODI series 2025 for FREE: live streams, schedule, teams
item.description: We'll show you all the ways you can watch New Zealand v England ODI live streams from anywhere in the world and potentially for free.
item.dc:creator: Roderick Easdale, TechRadar

item.title: Teen sues AI tool maker over fake nude images
item.description: A New Jersey teenager filed a major lawsuit against AI/Robotics Venture Strategy 3 Ltd. over ClothOff, an AI tool that created fake nude images from her social media photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your latest prescription is to get outside
item.description: Health care providers are trying a new tactic to promote the healing effects of nature: They're actually writing prescriptions for it.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here are 20-plus kid tools for higher-quality screen time
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Not everything creative needs a prompt. The web is increasingly flooded with AI-generated images and videos, much of it aimed at kids.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Scientists spot skyscraper-sized asteroid racing through solar system
item.description: Astronomers have discovered asteroid 2025 SC79, a skyscraper-sized space rock orbiting the sun in just 128 days. the second-fastest known.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why businesses banning AI inevitably lose
item.description: Those AI tools are being trained on our trade secrets. We’ll lose all of our customers if they find out our teams use AI. Our employees will no longer be able to think critically because of the brain rot caused by overreliance on AI. These are not irrational fears.
item.dc:creator: Konstantin Klyagin, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration pushes to get AI data centers on the grid faster
item.description: The Trump administration issued a proposal late Thursday that aims to help AI data centers get connected to the electric grid more quickly.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Applied Materials to cut more than 1,400 jobs after sales slow
item.description: Earlier this month, Applied Materials said an expansion of US export curbs to China would cost the company $600 million in lost revenue in fiscal 2026.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: Elon Musk literally left a government job with a black eye, has seen profits at his car company sink and must worry about federal regulators disrupting his plans to unleash a fleet of driverless taxis on America's roads in the next few years.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump calls off plans to deploy federal troops to San Francisco
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he would not deploy federal troops to San Francisco, reversing course on plans to implement anticrime and immigration enforcement in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: The hidden data problem killing enterprise AI projects
item.description: Headlines alternate between massive AI investments and reports of failed deployments. The pattern is consistent across industries: seemingly promising AI projects that work well in testing environments struggle or fail when deployed in real-world conditions.
item.dc:creator: Brian Moore, Fast Company

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A practitioner’s primer on deterministic application modernization
item.description: Large organizations rarely have just a handful of applications. They have thousands, often representing billions of lines of code. These code bases span decades of frameworks, libraries, and shifting best practices.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit sues Perplexity and others for allegedly scraping millions of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Solarium commission urges reversal on Trump’s cyber cuts
item.description: Cuts at CISA, the State Department and elsewhere have stalled, and in some cases reversed, progress on the Cyberspace Solarium Commission's recommendations. The post Solarium commission urges reversal on Trump’s cyber cuts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Reddit sues AI company Perplexity and others for 'industrial-scale' scraping of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kandji becomes Iru, opens MDM for Windows and Android
item.description: Apple device management and security company Kandji has changed its name to Iru, reflecting a new approach to what it does while opening its offer up to Windows and Android.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: AMD news and insights | Nvidia news and insights Intel is hoping for a turnaround under its new CEO, Lip-BuTan. Intel’s Q1 2025 revenue was $12.7 billion, flat year-over-year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel’s Panther Lake chip could allow Windows 11 to morph into an agentic OS
item.description: Intel’s upcoming “Panther Lake” processor for AI PCs could be a key building block in the transition of Windows 11 into an AI agent-driven OS, analysts said this week. Panther Lake will be available in PCs starting early in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China on Sunday accused the U.S. National Security Agency of carrying out cyberattacks on its national time center following an investigation, saying any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
